texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: POISSON APPROXIMATION OF PROCESSES WITH LOCALLY INDEPENDENT INCREMENTS WITH MARKOV SWITCHING
texts.text: POISSON APPROXIMATION OF PROCESSES WITH LOCALLY INDEPENDENT INCREMENTS WITH MARKOV SWITCHING

texts.label: text
texts.orig: V. S. Koroliuk 1 , N. Limnios 2 and I.V. Samoilenko 1
texts.text: V. S. Koroliuk 1 , N. Limnios 2 and I.V. Samoilenko 1

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Abstract. In this paper, the weak convergence of additive functionals of processes with locally independent increments and with Markov switching in the scheme of Poisson approximation is proved. For the relative compactness, a method proposed by R. Liptser for semimartingales is used with a modification, where we apply a solution of a singular perturbation problem instead of an ergodic theorem.
texts.text: Abstract. In this paper, the weak convergence of additive functionals of processes with locally independent increments and with Markov switching in the scheme of Poisson approximation is proved. For the relative compactness, a method proposed by R. Liptser for semimartingales is used with a modification, where we apply a solution of a singular perturbation problem instead of an ergodic theorem.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Poisson approximation is still an active area of research in several theoretical and applied directions. Several recent works on this topic can be found in the literature: we can find the classical approach in [1,2,3], and the functional approach in [8,9,7,12].
texts.text: Poisson approximation is still an active area of research in several theoretical and applied directions. Several recent works on this topic can be found in the literature: we can find the classical approach in [1,2,3], and the functional approach in [8,9,7,12].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In particular in [8,9] it has been studied the following stochastic additive functional
texts.text: In particular in [8,9] it has been studied the following stochastic additive functional

texts.label: text
texts.orig: of a jump Markov process with locally independent increments (PLII) ([8, page 14]) η ( t ; · ) , t ≥ 0, (also known as a Piecewise deterministic Markov process - PDMP, [5, Chapter 2]), perturbed by the jump Markov process x ( t ) , t ≥ 0. The process (1) is studied in a (functional) Poisson approximation scheme, within an ad hoc time-scaling as we can see below (2).
texts.text: of a jump Markov process with locally independent increments (PLII) ([8, page 14]) η ( t ; · ) , t ≥ 0, (also known as a Piecewise deterministic Markov process - PDMP, [5, Chapter 2]), perturbed by the jump Markov process x ( t ) , t ≥ 0. The process (1) is studied in a (functional) Poisson approximation scheme, within an ad hoc time-scaling as we can see below (2).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the Poisson approximation scheme, the jump values of the stochastic system are split into two parts: a small jump taking values with probabilities close to one and a big jump taken values with probabilities tending to zero together with the series parameter ε → 0. So, in the Poisson approximation principle the probabilities (or intensities) of jumps are normalized by the series parameter ε > 0. Hence the time-scaled family of processes ξ ε ( t ) , t ≥ 0 , ε > 0, has to be considered.
texts.text: In the Poisson approximation scheme, the jump values of the stochastic system are split into two parts: a small jump taking values with probabilities close to one and a big jump taken values with probabilities tending to zero together with the series parameter ε → 0. So, in the Poisson approximation principle the probabilities (or intensities) of jumps are normalized by the series parameter ε > 0. Hence the time-scaled family of processes ξ ε ( t ) , t ≥ 0 , ε > 0, has to be considered.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: However, the method used here to prove the weak convergence is quite different from the method proposed by other authors ([6]-[17]): the main point is to prove convergence of predictable characteristics of semimartingales which are integral functionals of some switching Markov processes. But the main difficulty is that the predictable characteristics of semimartingale themselves depend on the process we study. Thus, to prove the convergence of the process we should prove convergence of predictable characteristics that depend on the process. Ordinary methods can't help in this situation separately.
texts.text: However, the method used here to prove the weak convergence is quite different from the method proposed by other authors ([6]-[17]): the main point is to prove convergence of predictable characteristics of semimartingales which are integral functionals of some switching Markov processes. But the main difficulty is that the predictable characteristics of semimartingale themselves depend on the process we study. Thus, to prove the convergence of the process we should prove convergence of predictable characteristics that depend on the process. Ordinary methods can't help in this situation separately.

texts.orig: We propose to study functionals of PLII [8, page 14] using a combination of two methods. The method proposed by R. Liptser in [11], based on semimartingales theory, is combined with a solution of singular perturbation problem instead of ergodic theorem. So, the method includes two steps.
texts.text: We propose to study functionals of PLII [8, page 14] using a combination of two methods. The method proposed by R. Liptser in [11], based on semimartingales theory, is combined with a solution of singular perturbation problem instead of ergodic theorem. So, the method includes two steps.

texts.label: text

texts.orig: The paper is organized as follows. In Section 2 we present the time-scaled additive functional (1), the PLII and the switching Markov process. In the same section we present the main results of Poisson approximation. In Section 3 we present the proof of the theorem.
texts.text: The paper is organized as follows. In Section 2 we present the time-scaled additive functional (1), the PLII and the switching Markov process. In the same section we present the main results of Poisson approximation. In Section 3 we present the proof of the theorem.

texts.orig: We see from (10) and (12) that the solution of singular perturbation problem for L ε ϕ ε ( u, v ; x ) satisfies the conditions CD1, CD2 . Condition CD3 of this theorem implies that the quadratic characteristics of the martingale, corresponding to a coupled Markov process, is relatively compact. The same result follows from the CCC (see Corollary 2 and Lemma 2) by [7]. Thus, the condition CD3 follows from the Corollary 2 and Lemma 2. As soon as B ε (0) = B 0 (0) , ξ ε (0) = ξ 0 (0) we see that the condition CD4 is also satisfied. Thus, all the conditions of above Theorem 2 are satisfied, so the weak convergence B ε ( t ) ⇒ B 0 ( t ) takes place. By the same reasoning we can show the convergence of the processes C ε ( t ) and Γ ε ( t ) . The final step of the proof is achieved now by using Theorem IX.3.27 in [7]. Indeed all the conditions of this theorem are fulfilled.
texts.text: We see from (10) and (12) that the solution of singular perturbation problem for L ε ϕ ε ( u, v ; x ) satisfies the conditions CD1, CD2 . Condition CD3 of this theorem implies that the quadratic characteristics of the martingale, corresponding to a coupled Markov process, is relatively compact. The same result follows from the CCC (see Corollary 2 and Lemma 2) by [7]. Thus, the condition CD3 follows from the Corollary 2 and Lemma 2. As soon as B ε (0) = B 0 (0) , ξ ε (0) = ξ 0 (0) we see that the condition CD4 is also satisfied. Thus, all the conditions of above Theorem 2 are satisfied, so the weak convergence B ε ( t ) ⇒ B 0 ( t ) takes place. By the same reasoning we can show the convergence of the processes C ε ( t ) and Γ ε ( t ) . The final step of the proof is achieved now by using Theorem IX.3.27 in [7]. Indeed all the conditions of this theorem are fulfilled.
